title: Construction B binding operator
idea: The current deterministic explication->vector encoder: exact Hadamard XOR/TPR within a clause, whitened unitary HRR convolution across clauses/depth, fixed permutations for order, recursive concept-reference folding, superposition + normalise.
mechanism: Definition AST -> 8192-dim unit vector, training-free, byte-deterministic (encoder pin 40e8c8ba).
notes: The default encoder; X3 cosine-ban is its measured limitation (structural-overlap similarity).

title: Exact TPR binding (shallow)
idea: Alternative binding-op: exact tensor-product role-filler binding with orthonormal roles (zero crosstalk, exact unbind) for the shallow within-clause layer, accepting exponential blow-up only if kept shallow.
mechanism: Smolensky TPR within a clause; must compress across depth (which is what construction B already does via HRR).
notes: Deferred encoder-research alternative; construction B is already the compressed form of this.

title: WL-subtree features + compressed-sensing decode (construction C)
idea: Alternative binding-op: expand the explication into its WL-coloured subtree feature multiset, encode with a fixed sensing matrix, decode by L1 recovery.
mechanism: WL kernel + Arora-style compressed-sensing recovery; provable exact recovery at D=O(s log N).
notes: B and C are the same object; C is the formal lens for B's decodability.

title: Constructed box/hyperbolic taxonomy lane
idea: Add a deterministic geometric-entailment lane for the negation-free taxonomy fragment (EL-style subsumption as containment), constructed not learned.
mechanism: Sala combinatorial hyperbolic / Box2EL-style canonical construction over the concept dependency DAG, as an additive partitioned block (sparq SchemaHeader discipline).
notes: Regions honest only for the negation-free fragment; L1-survey open middle path.

title: Polarity-aware kernel similarity (X3 mitigation)
idea: A polarity/scope-aware similarity over kernel vectors so that a deep NOT is not near-invisible, lifting the X3 cosine ban for downstream similarity uses.
mechanism: notBoost-style polarity weighting or a decoded-structure comparator instead of raw cosine.
notes: ENABLER: any similarity-based idea (differentiable proving, kernel-kNN, soft unification) is blocked until this passes. Encoder-side, CPU, cheap.

title: Structural-decode similarity
idea: Compute meaning-level comparison over the DECODED structure (shared primes/subtrees relationally), never over the raw vector metric.
mechanism: Decode-then-compare using the kernel as cleanup memory; the E2 RDM instrument is the relational form.
notes: Sibling of idea-polarity-similarity in the X3-mitigation family.

title: AND-under-operator grammar extension (X1)
idea: Add an n-ary ClauseGroup node so conjunction can sit under an operator, unlocking disjunction via de Morgan and non-OTHER cardinality.
mechanism: kot-ast/2 ClauseGroup; encoder + identity version bump (ALGORITHM_VERSION, X0 regen).
notes: Decide only when a concrete blocked axiom class arrives (DL fork F5 kill: <20% need it).

title: Apply-clause grammar extension (X5)
idea: Add apply-clauses binding RelationalSchema concepts against their declared signature, so relations can be referenced not inlined (preserves the capacity budget).
mechanism: kot-ast/2 apply node; highest-value grammar extension per the DL analysis.
notes: Feeds L3a axiom encoding (litmus rule needs it); relational reuse frequency decides it (fork F6).

title: Vocabulary/basis extensions (molecule, physics, math, code)
idea: Extend the atomic basis beyond NSM-65: molecule tier, physics Z^7 dimension lane, math Metamath codebook, and code/structured constructs (feeds idea A).
mechanism: Additive typed lanes/codebook swaps; physics Z^7 exact integer, math via codebook+valency swap, code constructs new.
notes: physics/math lanes planned-not-built; code-constructs is a prerequisite for idea-structured-data-parser.

title: Axiom-layer expressivity (kot-axiom v0 vs +rules)
idea: The kot-axiom/1 sidecar expressivity: v0 validation operators (lookup/inverse/functional/count/disjoint) vs a bounded rule layer.
mechanism: Closed validation semantics as query operators; +Datalog-style bounded evaluation as engineering, semantics native.
notes: A fork WITHIN L3a; expressivity decided by L3a's missed-query failure analysis.

title: World-layer population route
idea: How stratum-4 fact records get authored: deterministic extraction from structured sources, LLM-authored+validator-gated, or hybrid.
mechanism: Provenance-stamped record authoring; authoring cost + record-error rate land on metric-vector V.
notes: A fork WITHIN L3a; also gates f2b-TRANSFER's record-backed external items (fact-coverage).

title: A2 trained-adapter injection
idea: Kernel vector -> trained affine adapter -> soft token into a frozen host; the one supported in-network injection cell.
mechanism: Deterministic kernel vector, per-model trained bridge; E5 PASS (content transfers to nonce concepts +28.5pp, 1 rung).
notes: Supported at one rung (135M), content-transfer only; no end-task-vs-text-null verdict yet.

title: Concept-toolkens (frozen kernel-derived embeddings)
idea: ~10^3 new vocabulary items whose embeddings are frozen kernel-derived vectors, with only one shared adapter trained (ToolkenGPT inverted).
mechanism: Frozen kernel vectors as toolken embeddings; the cheapest unrun A2 experiment.
notes: Cheap, direct, unrun; must beat the in-context-text null (Law 2).

title: External deterministic verifier seam (A5)
idea: generate -> P10 extraction -> deterministic decode-verify against canonical records + axiom sidecar -> retry.
mechanism: Tool-in-loop (Law-3 topology), deterministic checker not similarity search; F2 verdict FAIL on a degenerate primary, secondaries positive but MEASURED-UNAUDITED.
notes: f2b-REPLICATE (absolute non-inferiority primary + separation gate + shuffled-kernel control) is the live re-test; the oracle-leakage caveat gates the D-QA lift.

title: A1 frozen-vocabulary rows
idea: Kernel vectors as native frozen model I/O rows, pretrained around; zero trained interface.
mechanism: JL-projected kernel rows frozen in the embedding table.
notes: Law-1 empty cell; retained only as gated cheap falsification. A learned projection inside it silently becomes A2 (forbidden).

title: A6 kernel<->SAE label space
idea: Kernel as a canonical, versioned label/coordinate system for learned SAE features.
mechanism: Alignment map between kernel geometry and SAE feature directions; instrument over model internals.
notes: 2025 SAE correction (absorption, seed non-identifiability) is the opening; GDM bar = beat a simple baseline on a downstream task.

title: L1a kernel input canonicaliser
idea: Deterministic canonicalisation/annotation of kernel-covered input text before the model; training-free, external.
mechanism: Mapper (phrase->concept, a1-hybrid) rewrites/annotates covered spans to one canonical surface form; measured as variance reduction under paraphrase.
notes: Cheapest model-facing rung; must beat the LLM-paraphrase-normaliser null; abstention rate bounds coverage.

title: L1b concept-dense I/O + record-constrained output
idea: Kernel-covered spans enter as dense concept tokens; kernel-covered answers leave as P10 records rendered to canonical text.
mechanism: X4-projected kernel vectors through the E5 adapter (input); record-constrained decode (output); cuts prefill/KV and decode steps.
notes: Must avoid the LCM/SONAR fixed-space exponent penalty (edge-only, not internal target); scaling-exponent tripwire if trained beyond the adapter.

title: L2a kernel-labelled concept bottleneck
idea: A mid-network layer over a frozen host whose units are pinned 1:1 to kernel concept hashes (CB-LLM recipe, canonical vocabulary).
mechanism: Bottleneck head with versioned, content-addressed, seed-stable unit identities; merges A2+A6.
notes: Concept leakage is the known failure mode (measure with scramble arm); strongest interp-lane rung / A6 pivot.

title: L2b kernel-addressed in-network memory layer
idea: In-FFN product-key memory layer with a fraction of the key table pinned to kernel concept identities, values learned.
mechanism: Pinned keys (X4-projected or hash-derived) give per-token concept attribution + versioned edit-locality; values remain learned.
notes: Empty literature cell; frozen-embedding record predicts a pinned-key penalty (a fork to test, shuffled-kernel-keys mandatory).

title: L2c semantic-fixedness sweep (phi)
idea: Sweep phi = k/D, the fraction of the representation subspace pinned to kernel vectors, as a dose-response crossed with scale and in/out-of-kernel domain; includes the phi=1 LCM-analog endpoint.
mechanism: Seeded orthonormal pinned block, gradient-masked; phi in {0,.25,.5,.75,1}; de-confounds the LCM penalty (definitional vs sentence, partial vs comprehensive).
notes: PARAMETRIC cross-cutting axis; one experiment de-confounds the LCM anchor that gates the whole L1b/L2a/L2b family. Lite (frozen host) ready; full (exponent leg) Tier-4 double-gated.

title: L3a rules-engine oracle
idea: kot-axiom engine + world-layer index answers covered factual queries alone (no LLM in loop), with provenance and axiom-licensed refusal, at orders-of-magnitude lower cost.
mechanism: Deterministic mapper parse -> query grammar over kot-axiom relations -> unique-answer index lookup; e.g. mother(Elvis)=Gladys in microseconds on CPU.
notes: In progress. Cheapest-decisive; builds the kot-axiom engine owed anyway; also underpins idea-symbolic-inference-between-layers (its rules step).

title: L3b routed hybrid inference
idea: Deterministic router sends covered queries to the engine and everything else to the LLM; the deployable form.
mechanism: mapper-confidence + query-grammar match => engine; else => LLM; mis-route rate is a first-class gate.
notes: Composes with (not against) the verifier seam; must beat LLM+RAG-over-facts at matched cost.

title: L3c engine-in-decode
idea: Engine invoked mid-generation; covered spans constrained-decoded from engine-supplied records (deterministic speculative decoding).
mechanism: P10-in-reverse + decode-time integration; engine drafts the fact, model drafts the prose.
notes: Horizon of the L3 family; only worth designing if L3b passes and covered facts occur inside long generations.

title: CALM-hybrid: latent regularised toward kernel coordinates
idea: A CALM-style learned autoencoder latent softly regularised toward kernel coordinates on covered concepts (regulariser, not target).
mechanism: Learned reconstruction-optimised latent + a lambda pull toward canonical axes for covered concepts; the one untested middle path between fixed-target and free latent.
notes: Scaling-exponent kill mandatory (LCM/CALM named anchor); any measurable exponent degradation vs lambda=0 kills.

title: Sidecar-axiom / kernel-RDM training regularisers
idea: Render kot-axiom axioms as differentiable penalties, and use the kernel RDM/Gram as a relational anchor, during adapter/distillation training.
mechanism: LTN/semantic-loss over axioms; RDM-level (relational, X3-compliant) anchor during distillation.
notes: Cheap rider: add as arms to F4/F6 rather than new experiments.

title: Gradual-introduction training schedule
idea: Introduce kernel structure progressively over training (curriculum over phi, coverage, or regulariser weight) to avoid the frozen-embedding convergence penalty.
mechanism: Schedule that ramps kernel fixedness/coverage across steps; hypothesised to mitigate the 5-10x mismatched-frozen-embedding convergence cost.
notes: Named training-schedule axis (Jesse); crosses phi (L2c) as a time dimension; training-side so deferred.

title: Kernel as MoE expert-label instrument
idea: Probe open MoE routers/experts against mapper-detected kernel concepts (do experts cluster on kernel concepts beyond controls?).
mechanism: e8-style alignment over MoE experts instead of SAE features; instrument-first (consumer-side routing change is Law-1-exposed).
notes: Inference-only rider on the e8 machinery; needs >=2 MoE families to beat token-identity + shuffled controls.

title: Deterministic structured-data -> concept parsing (idea A)
idea: For structured input/output (code, math/Lean, config), a CODE-based deterministic parser maps structure to kernel concepts at inference time, extending the proven Lean->kernel math pipeline; hypothesised to generalise to most programming languages.
mechanism: Deterministic AST/grammar parser (not an LLM) at the encoder input edge and decoder output edge; benchmark = code-understanding + code-writing with vs without the parser built into encoder/decoder.
notes: SUB-PROGRAMME candidate: warrants its own benchmark. NOW slice = math/Lean (precedent exists, cheap); DEFERRED = general-code generalisation (needs the benchmark build + code-construct vocabulary). The generalise-to-most-PLs claim is STIPULATED and must be registered as an assumption before any prereg premises it.

title: Symbolic inference between layers (idea B)
idea: An inter-layer op: decode the hidden-state vector -> concepts (kernel), run symbolic inference over that concept set (rules-engine), re-encode resulting concepts -> vector -> next layer; variant = a dedicated hidden layer.
mechanism: Per-layer decode -> kot-axiom rules-engine step -> re-encode; the rules step is L3a's engine placed inside the forward pass.
notes: Highest-decisiveness, highest-uncertainty. Blocked until L3a's engine exists AND model-side hidden-state decode is reliable (X2 was encoder-side only) AND X3 is mitigated. Naturally sequences AFTER L3a. Law-1 exposed (modifies the forward pass). FAMILY (Line-4 refinement): idea-inter-layer-loop-trainingfree (B1, the buildable training-free composition), idea-trained-kernel-bridge-depth (B4, the trained single-group occupant), idea-backpatch-diagnostic (B5, the cheap pre-experiment/ceiling), idea-coconut-engine-loop (B6, horizon). Run B5 before B1.

title: Structured-input canonicaliser for code/tables (A1)
idea: L1a extended beyond NL: deterministically canonicalise code/table input (format, identifiers, serialization) to one canonical surface form before the model.
mechanism: tree-sitter/format parsers -> canonical surface form; endpoint = output variance under ReCode/table-perturbation at accuracy non-inferiority.
notes: Report A1. Code sibling of idea-l1a-canonicaliser; share the canonicaliser design. Motivators arXiv:2412.17189/2605.29676 are [claimed] (aggregate-accuracy effect may be small) — variance is the honest endpoint; kernel arm EXTRAPOLATION until a verdict.

title: Grammar+type-constrained P10-record emission, two-stage decouple (A2)
idea: Compile kot-record/kot-axiom grammars to XGrammar-class token masks so kernel-record output is well-formed by construction; reason freely first, then constrained-encode.
mechanism: PICARD/SynCode/type-constrained decoding at ~zero overhead (XGrammar); directly hardens the F2/E9 extraction-failure gate and the L1b output leg / L3a query grammar / L3c.
notes: Report A2. Components all [established]; composition EXTRAPOLATION. The two-stage-decouple ARM rests on [claimed] follow-ups (arXiv:2604.03616/2603.03305) so it is an arm, not a premise; carry it to avoid misattributing the format tax (arXiv:2408.02442 [established]) to the kernel. Distribution-distortion caveat (GAD) must be priced.

title: Trie-constrained concept-name emission + name->hash map (A3)
idea: GENRE-shape: constrain decoding to a prefix trie over the registered concept lexicon; hashes never enter the model; a deterministic name->hash map finishes.
mechanism: Trie-constrained beam search over concept names + exact post-map; rescues the E4 hash-emission null (which failed as a TRAINING target; name emission needs no training).
notes: Report A3. Mechanism [established]; kernel use EXTRAPOLATION. Directly rescues the E4 emission null via a no-training path.

title: Kernel-record paired-corpus grounding, IRCoder shape (A4)
idea: Continued pretraining of small hosts on text<->canonical-kernel-record pairs; the concrete corpus recipe F6/N-C2 was missing.
mechanism: IRCoder/StructCoder-shape paired corpus; endpoints incl. prompt robustness (IRCoder's measured gain at 1.1B-7.3B).
notes: Report A4. Precedent [established] at 1.1B-7.3B; kernel transfer EXTRAPOLATION. Absorption expected (frame as training-efficiency).

title: Code world-layer via deterministic CPG/call-graph extraction (A5)
idea: Deterministic Joern/tree-sitter code-property-graph extraction populates a code-domain world layer that the L3a engine queries; answers structure queries LLMs measurably fail at.
mechanism: CPG/call-graph -> world-layer records -> engine answers call-graph/AST/data-flow queries; division-of-labour (engine owns structure, LLM owns everything else).
notes: Report A5. X3 TRAP (restate at freeze): mapping extracted code structures to kernel concepts must be EXACT (content-hash/mapper), never kernel-space nearest-neighbour. In code the Law-3 engine seat is held by COMPILERS/analysers; the kernel's marginal value is canonical concept IDENTITY, not re-doing static analysis. Kernel delta EXTRAPOLATION. L3a code vertical / fast-follow behind the engine.

title: Kernel-canonical shallow facts as prompt augmentation, text (A6)
idea: Deterministic shallow facts (names, return exprs, simple pre/post-conditions) rendered as TEXT into the prompt; the only input-side cell that still pays at LLM scale.
mechanism: ASAP-shape shallow-fact augmentation; kernel delta = canonical identity/versioning of the facts vs the same facts ad-hoc (measured against unversioned).
notes: Report A6. Law-2-native (facts enter as text). +~2 BLEU precedent [established]; kernel delta (canonicality/versioning) EXTRAPOLATION. Runs as an arm of idea-l1a-canonicaliser.

title: Training-free inter-layer loop (B1)
idea: At a chosen layer: patchscope-decode hidden state -> mapper-exact concept identification -> kot-axiom engine -> feedback (text / cached steering vector / back-patch). The training-free, buildable form of idea-symbolic-inference-between-layers.
mechanism: All-established instruments composed; concept ID is EXACT-match on decoded text (X3-respected, no kernel-space kNN); nulls = F2-external loop at matched budget + shuffled-kernel + scramble.
notes: Report B1. The empty motivated cell (new rung L1.5 between L0 and L2). Composition is EXTRAPOLATION and premises nothing until it runs. Hazards (report 2.4): extraction validity (Faithful-Patchscopes [claimed]), SATNet grounding-leakage, steering-reliability, Law-2 (F2-input null), absorption. Refines idea-symbolic-inference-between-layers; run B5 FIRST.

title: Kernel-keyed steering dictionary (B2)
idea: concept-hash -> per-model model-native function/steering vector (extracted once by causal mediation, cached, exact-hash addressed); the write channel of B1 without a trained adapter.
mechanism: Function-vector extraction over the model's OWN activations; injection = activation addition; kernel coordinates never enter the model (Law-1-clean).
notes: Report B2. Mechanism [established]; concept-scale coverage EXTRAPOLATION. Steering-eval fragility documented (arXiv:2410.17245 [claimed]). Defer until B5/B1 show inter-layer value is real.

title: VSA-algebra rule evaluation inside the kot-axiom engine (B3)
idea: Evaluate sidecar rules by TPR/HRR binding/unbinding over kernel vectors (NVSA shape) for speed; an IMPLEMENTATION of L3a, not a new seam.
mechanism: In-algebra rule evaluation (NVSA measured ~100x cheaper than symbolic search on its domain); conditional on X3-safe cleanup.
notes: Report B3. X3 TRAP (restate at freeze): NVSA pipelines end in a nearest-neighbour cleanup memory, which is X3-BANNED for kernel vectors; any adoption must replace cleanup-by-similarity with EXACT content-hash lookup (or a passed polarity-aware variant). Port EXTRAPOLATION; blocked until X3-safe cleanup exists.

title: Trained kernel bridge at depth (2502.01657 replication) (B4)
idea: Replicate the trained hidden-state<->kernel-space bridge at one layer (symbolic compute, 50-50 merge back), replacing ad-hoc VSA with kernel space, and RUN the missing engine-as-text-input null the original lacked.
mechanism: Trained encoder/decoder at a chosen layer; Law-1-compliant (trained bridge); sibling of idea-l2a-bottleneck.
notes: Report B4. MOTIVATOR arXiv:2502.01657 is [claimed] SINGLE-GROUP UNREPLICATED and MAY NOT premise a prereg (coordinator discipline). Treat as motivation only; the replication MUST add the engine-as-text null (Law 2) the original omitted. Kernel variant EXTRAPOLATION.

title: Back-patching diagnostic: are covered-slice failures layer-timing failures? (B5)
idea: Replicate the back-patch sweep on kernel-covered multi-hop slices; the fraction rescuable = a CEILING estimate on inter-layer intervention value, informing L2 placement and B1's write-channel choice.
mechanism: Move a later-layer hidden state back to an earlier layer; measure fraction of covered-slice failures rescued (general 2-hop: up to 66% rescuable, measured).
notes: Report B5. Method [established]. The CHEAP pre-experiment BEFORE B1 and before expensive L2 rungs: a low fraction-rescuable on covered slices cheaply deprioritises the whole inter-layer/B1/B6/L2 family. Open weights + forward hooks; cheapest-decisive-first.

title: Coconut-wiring + deterministic engine + no training (B6)
idea: Coconut's hidden-state->input-embedding loop with the kot-axiom engine interposed and NO training; the far ambition of B1.
mechanism: Latent loop with a deterministic engine in it; nobody has run the training-free variant.
notes: Report B6. Line [established] for TRAINED versions only; this training-free cell is EXTRAPOLATION, empty. Horizon; gate strictly on B1. NOT proposed for prereg.

title: BOUNDARY: serialized-AST / deep-static-analysis input injection at LLM scale (X1)
idea: Non-adoption boundary condition: handing an LLM a serialized AST/deep-static-analysis of code it can already read is parity, and static-analysis pretraining does not transfer in.
mechanism: Measured parity (2602.06671) + no-transfer (2505.12118); do NOT spend budget on AST-serialization INPUT arms except as controls.

compatibility.conflicts_note: soft: canonicalisation-for-variance survives; raw-AST-for-accuracy does not

notes: Report X1. LIT-BACKED NEGATIVE. Same absorption/interface-locality pattern as the KEPLM line. A constraint on the map, not a test: encoder-side input AST injection at LLM scale is a control arm at most. Note idea-code-input-canonicaliser survives on the VARIANCE endpoint (not accuracy).

idea: Deterministic Joern/tree-sitter code-property-graph extraction populates a code-domain world layer that the L3a engine queries; answers structure queries LLMs measurably fail at.
mechanism: CPG/call-graph -> world-layer records -> engine answers call-graph/AST/data-flow queries; division-of-labour (engine owns structure, LLM owns everything else).
notes: Report A5. X3 TRAP (restate at freeze): mapping extracted code structures to kernel concepts must be EXACT (content-hash/mapper), never kernel-space nearest-neighbour. In code the Law-3 engine seat is held by COMPILERS/analysers; the kernel's marginal value is canonical concept IDENTITY, not re-doing static analysis. Kernel delta EXTRAPOLATION. L3a code vertical / fast-follow behind the engine. PROMOTED 2026-07-09: pre-registered + run as registry id a5 (frozen 6c42f8a8; X3 exact-hash trap restated at freeze per this note - construct->concept, axiom, and op->relation tables are all exact minted-URN lookups); verdict PASS (cross-vendor audit CONFIRMED 2026-07-09, registry/audits/a5/1-gate-a-codex.json; scope = engine leg on this pinned slice only); epistemic flip to MEASURED happens at idea assessment, not here.
title: Code world-layer via deterministic CPG/call-graph extraction (A5)
Cross-lingual common-phrase concept coverage + cached-context Haiku concept-definer, for LLM I/O compression (maintainer-proposed; Fable to design)
idea: Mint concepts for the highest-frequency words/phrases ACROSS many languages (statistically selected) so the encoder can COMPRESS LLM input into few dense concept tokens and the decoder can express high-bandwidth content in very few LLM tokens; generate the concept definitions cheaply via a Haiku agent whose entire how-to-define-a-word-as-a-concept context is CACHED, leaving only the single target word as variable input and a tiny (ideally directly-symbolic) output.
mechanism: Two coupled moves. (1) CHEAP MINT: a Haiku concept-definer with a fixed cached prefix (the full 'how to define an English word as a concept' instruction) + only the single word as variable input => input tokens are cache-hits, output is tiny, especially if a symbolic explication is emitted directly. (2) COVERAGE-MAXIMISING SELECTION: cross-lingual frequency statistics pick which words/phrases MUST exist as concepts so that a large fraction of real text is coverable by single concept-tokens. Net intended effect = fewer LLM tokens in and out = cheaper inference. This is the programme-2 architecture-ladder 'kernel-in-tokenisation/normalisation' rung specialised into a coverage-maximising, cross-lingual, cheaply-minted ALTERNATIVE kernel.

compatibility.conflicts_note: Must beat the kernel-as-text null and confront the LCM fixed-semantic-IO scaling penalty (Law 2); the compression benefit must survive the trained-bridge overhead (Law 1 interface-locality).

open_questions: FORK (maintainer-flagged): does asking Haiku for a DIRECTLY SYMBOLIC output reduce output tokens, or paradoxically INCREASE thinking-time/latency? decisive cheap sub-test before committing.
Does concept-token compression actually reduce END-TO-END cost once encoder/decoder + trained-bridge overhead is counted (vs BPE tokeniser baseline + kernel-as-text null)?
Cross-lingual phrase-selection method (which frequency corpora / alignment; per-language vs shared concept space).
Coverage vs LCM/fixed-semantic-IO scaling penalty: at high coverage does accuracy degrade with scale (the phi-fixedness axis)?
notes: Maintainer (Jesse) 2026-07-09: DO NOT BUILD YET — Fable must create AND sign off the design first. Value = EFFICIENCY thesis (I/O compression -> cheap inference), not accuracy. Generates an ALTERNATIVE kernel via a cheap cached-prefix Haiku process. Cross-links: programme-2 architecture ladder in-tokenisation/normalisation rung + the phi-semantic-fixedness sweep + the kernel-as-text null (Law 2).

title: Encoder/decoder static-analysis roll-up of sentence structure into invented composite concepts (maintainer-proposed; Fable to design)
idea: The encoder does static/symbolic analysis to ROLL UP recognisable compositional structure ('a is b', 'a AND b', 'a OR c') into a single INVENTED composite concept whose vector is DETERMINISTICALLY derived from its parts, so those tokens reach the LLM as one concept; the decoder carries a per-utterance dictionary of the invented concepts to expand them losslessly when it must state them.
mechanism: The kernel already binds clauses via exact Hadamard TPR + HRR convolution, so a composite like 'a AND b' HAS a deterministically-derivable vector from its parts. Apply that binding at ENCODE time to collapse recognised connective/copula structures (AND/OR/is-a) into fewer concept tokens; pass a per-utterance invented-concept dictionary to the decoder for lossless expansion. Family: deterministic structured-data->concept parsing + symbolic composition between layers.

compatibility.conflicts_note: MUST respect idea-ast-input-injection-boundary (X1): injecting structure the LLM can already read is PARITY on ACCURACY and static-analysis does not transfer in. So this idea is justified ONLY on the COMPRESSION/EFFICIENCY endpoint (fewer tokens, like idea-code-input-canonicaliser survives on VARIANCE) — never as an accuracy-from-structure claim.

open_questions: Which compositional patterns are safely + deterministically roll-uppable (AND/OR/is-a/copula) without meaning loss?
Does the composite vector (from the existing binding algebra) actually carry the meaning into the LLM through the trained bridge (Law 1)?
Lossless decode via the invented-concept dictionary — round-trip fidelity.
How much real text is captured by these patterns (coverage -> achievable compression ratio)?
Net effect on model accuracy at matched budget vs the kernel-as-text null.
notes: Maintainer (Jesse) 2026-07-09, framed as SEPARATE from kernel design (an encoder/decoder architecture). DO NOT BUILD YET — Fable designs + signs off. Honesty linkage: constrained by idea-ast-input-injection-boundary (X1) — frame as compression/efficiency, NOT accuracy-from-structure. Reuses the existing deterministic binding algebra for composite vectors.

title: Cross-lingual common-phrase concept coverage + cached-context Haiku concept-definer, for LLM I/O compression (maintainer-proposed; Fable to design)
idea: Mint concepts for the highest-frequency words/phrases ACROSS many languages (statistically selected) so the encoder can COMPRESS LLM input into few dense concept tokens and the decoder can express high-bandwidth content in very few LLM tokens; generate the concept definitions cheaply via a Haiku agent whose entire how-to-define-a-word-as-a-concept context is CACHED, leaving only the single target word as variable input and a tiny (ideally directly-symbolic) output.
mechanism: Two coupled moves. (1) CHEAP MINT: a Haiku concept-definer with a fixed cached prefix (the full 'how to define an English word as a concept' instruction) + only the single word as variable input => input tokens are cache-hits, output is tiny, especially if a symbolic explication is emitted directly. (2) COVERAGE-MAXIMISING SELECTION: cross-lingual frequency statistics pick which words/phrases MUST exist as concepts so that a large fraction of real text is coverable by single concept-tokens. Net intended effect = fewer LLM tokens in and out = cheaper inference. This is the programme-2 architecture-ladder 'kernel-in-tokenisation/normalisation' rung specialised into a coverage-maximising, cross-lingual, cheaply-minted ALTERNATIVE kernel.

compatibility.conflicts_note: Must beat the kernel-as-text null and confront the LCM fixed-semantic-IO scaling penalty (Law 2); the compression benefit must survive the trained-bridge overhead (Law 1 interface-locality).

open_questions: FORK (maintainer-flagged): does asking Haiku for a DIRECTLY SYMBOLIC output reduce output tokens, or paradoxically INCREASE thinking-time/latency? decisive cheap sub-test before committing.
Does concept-token compression actually reduce END-TO-END cost once encoder/decoder + trained-bridge overhead is counted (vs BPE tokeniser baseline + kernel-as-text null)?
Cross-lingual phrase-selection method (which frequency corpora / alignment; per-language vs shared concept space).
Coverage vs LCM/fixed-semantic-IO scaling penalty: at high coverage does accuracy degrade with scale (the phi-fixedness axis)?
notes: Maintainer (Jesse) 2026-07-09: DO NOT BUILD YET — Fable must create AND sign off the design first. Value = EFFICIENCY thesis (I/O compression -> cheap inference), not accuracy. Generates an ALTERNATIVE kernel via a cheap cached-prefix Haiku process. Cross-links: programme-2 architecture ladder in-tokenisation/normalisation rung + the phi-semantic-fixedness sweep + the kernel-as-text null (Law 2). CORRECTION (Fable, 2026-07-09, bead kernel-of-truth-utq): dangling reference idea-a2-adapter-injection fixed to the defined id idea-a2-adapter (E5 PASS adapter-injection seam); appended line supersedes per last-line-wins.

title: Encoder/decoder static-analysis roll-up of sentence structure into invented composite concepts (maintainer-proposed; Fable to design)
idea: The encoder does static/symbolic analysis to ROLL UP recognisable compositional structure ('a is b', 'a AND b', 'a OR c') into a single INVENTED composite concept whose vector is DETERMINISTICALLY derived from its parts, so those tokens reach the LLM as one concept; the decoder carries a per-utterance dictionary of the invented concepts to expand them losslessly when it must state them.
mechanism: The kernel already binds clauses via exact Hadamard TPR + HRR convolution, so a composite like 'a AND b' HAS a deterministically-derivable vector from its parts. Apply that binding at ENCODE time to collapse recognised connective/copula structures (AND/OR/is-a) into fewer concept tokens; pass a per-utterance invented-concept dictionary to the decoder for lossless expansion. Family: deterministic structured-data->concept parsing + symbolic composition between layers.

compatibility.conflicts_note: MUST respect idea-ast-input-injection-boundary (X1): injecting structure the LLM can already read is PARITY on ACCURACY and static-analysis does not transfer in. So this idea is justified ONLY on the COMPRESSION/EFFICIENCY endpoint (fewer tokens, like idea-code-input-canonicaliser survives on VARIANCE) — never as an accuracy-from-structure claim.

open_questions: Which compositional patterns are safely + deterministically roll-uppable (AND/OR/is-a/copula) without meaning loss?
Does the composite vector (from the existing binding algebra) actually carry the meaning into the LLM through the trained bridge (Law 1)?
Lossless decode via the invented-concept dictionary — round-trip fidelity.
How much real text is captured by these patterns (coverage -> achievable compression ratio)?
Net effect on model accuracy at matched budget vs the kernel-as-text null.
notes: Maintainer (Jesse) 2026-07-09, framed as SEPARATE from kernel design (an encoder/decoder architecture). DO NOT BUILD YET — Fable designs + signs off. Honesty linkage: constrained by idea-ast-input-injection-boundary (X1) — frame as compression/efficiency, NOT accuracy-from-structure. Reuses the existing deterministic binding algebra for composite vectors. CORRECTION (Fable, 2026-07-09, bead kernel-of-truth-utq): dangling reference idea-a2-adapter-injection fixed to the defined id idea-a2-adapter (E5 PASS adapter-injection seam); appended line supersedes per last-line-wins.

title: Standard LLM-leaderboard benchmark evaluation of kernel AUGMENTATION on the covered slice, coverage-gated (maintainer-proposed; Fable to place in the ladder)
idea: Evaluate 'how good the best kernel support actually is' against the benchmarks used on LLM leaderboards (MMLU, GSM8K, ARC, HellaSwag, TruthfulQA, etc.), but as an AUGMENTATION value-add measurement on the kernel-COVERED subset of each benchmark at matched compute — plus tracking how the covered slice grows as coverage grows — NOT as a naive 'kernel vs leaderboard' score.
mechanism: For each leaderboard benchmark, partition items into kernel_checkable (covered) vs not. On the covered subset, measure whether kernel-augmentation (verify-retry / in-tokenisation / grounding) improves accuracy at matched FLOPs vs model-alone; report the covered fraction alongside every number (coverage-disclosure discipline). Re-run as coverage grows (kernel-variant expansion, haiku-tier, structured-DB ingestion) to chart the applicable-slice trajectory. Ties to the existing efficiency track (accuracy x size x memory x compute) and the m0b coverage instrument.

compatibility.conflicts_note: Premature at current coverage (0.3542, incomplete kernel-v0): a leaderboard run today mostly measures coverage, not kernel quality, and near-zero engagement would read as misleadingly 'bad'. Value theses are CORRECTNESS + EFFICIENCY, not broad-knowledge leaderboard-beating; frame as augmentation value-add on the covered slice + coverage-growth trajectory.

open_questions: At what coverage milestone does a leaderboard run become informative rather than coverage-dominated?
Which benchmarks have a non-trivial kernel-expressible subset (definitional/lexical/entailment items) vs essentially none (broad world trivia)?
Augmentation mode to test on the covered slice: verify-retry vs in-tokenisation vs grounding-lint.
How to report so a small covered-slice win is not oversold as a general leaderboard result.
notes: Maintainer (Jesse) 2026-07-09: wants to know how good the BEST kernel support does vs LLM-leaderboard benchmarks; asked to build it into the plan at an appropriate point. Fable to PLACE it in the ladder/roadmap as a coverage-gated milestone tied to the efficiency track, with the covered-slice + coverage-disclosure framing. Do NOT run at current coverage.

title: Kernel as a grounding/precision guardrail + controlled-language LINTER for LLM output (maintainer-proposed; Fable to research+design)
idea: Use the kernel as, or as part of, a framework that guardrails / 'lints' LLM outputs to follow a very precise linguistic style — producing clear, concise, precisely-articulated written language with no meaningless or hallucinated text — by grounding every proposition in the kernel's controlled, NSM-grounded vocabulary and flagging/rewriting what cannot be grounded.
mechanism: Parse LLM output into propositions; check each for kernel-expressibility (the existing kernel_checkable machinery + the encoder/decoder); FLAG or REJECT propositions that cannot be grounded (candidate vagueness / meaningless filler / unverifiable-against-kernel = potential hallucination surface) and optionally REWRITE the groundable content into a clear, concise, kernel-grounded controlled-language paraphrase (NSM reductive-paraphrase style). Family: controlled natural language (e.g. Attempto ACE) + NSM reductive paraphrase + LLM guardrails/constrained decoding, built on the kernel's deterministic grounded vocabulary.

compatibility.conflicts_note: Plays to the kernel's ACTUAL strength (grounded, deterministic, precise vocabulary; NSM IS a reductive-paraphrase system) — arguably a stronger value proposition than verifier-offload. Honesty caveat: ungroundable != false; at current tiny coverage the linter would flag most text as ungrounded, so full utility scales with coverage — but the CONCEPT (grounding guardrail + controlled-language rewrite) is designable now. Possible synergy with the maintainer's separate Shuttle/SHACL-CS precise-linguistic-formalism programme.

open_questions: Operational definition of 'lint': flag-only vs reject vs rewrite; what counts as 'meaningless' vs 'ungroundable-but-meaningful'?
How to distinguish hallucination from merely-out-of-coverage (ungroundable != false) without over-flagging at low coverage?
Controlled-language target: NSM reductive paraphrase vs a CNL like ACE vs a kernel-native surface — round-trip fidelity + readability.
Where does this sit vs programme-2 (in-normalisation rung) and the Shuttle/SHACL-CS programme?
Evaluation: precision/recall of the hallucination/vagueness flags; human-rated clarity/concision of the rewritten text; does it actually reduce meaningless/unfaithful output?
notes: Maintainer (Jesse) 2026-07-09: wants the kernel usable as a guardrail/linter for very precise written language (clear, concise, no meaningless/hallucinated text). HIGH interest, potentially reframes the kernel's value prop toward grounding/precision (its real strength) rather than leaderboard-beating. Fable to do a LITERATURE-GROUNDED design proposal (NSM reductive paraphrase + CNL + guardrails), place in the idea-registry with compatibility links to programme-2 and the Shuttle/SHACL-CS programme.

title: Leaderboard-benchmark evaluation of kernel AUGMENTATION on the checkable slice, coverage-gated — SCOPED as N1-LB (architecture-ladder §10): b-cov census (Tier 0, now) + LB-1 covered-slice eval (Tier 1-2, LB-GATE)
idea: Measure 'how good the best kernel support actually is' on LLM-leaderboard benchmarks as an AUGMENTATION value-add on the kernel-CHECKABLE subset of each benchmark at matched compute, plus a per-benchmark checkable-slice-growth trajectory across kernel versions — never a naive blended 'kernel vs leaderboard' score. Two artefacts: (1) b-cov, a deterministic Tier-0 benchmark-checkability census (m0b's sibling; per-benchmark lemma-touch sieve + mode-indexed kernel_checkable fractions κ_B^verify / κ_B^engine, keyed to kernel/world-layer/mapper/grammar hashes, appended per kernel version); (2) LB-1, an f2b-TRANSFER-shaped covered-slice eval on named public benchmarks, gated by LB-GATE.
mechanism: b-cov census: per pinned benchmark, compute the d-ext R-C lemma-touch sieve and the runtime verifier's own kernel_checkable predicate (plus the L3 mapper+closed-grammar parse fraction) without any model call; log the trajectory per kernel version. LB-1: on the census-checkable slice only, arms {model-alone; kernel-verify-retry (only if f2b-TRANSFER PASSes); shuffled-kernel-verify; kernel-as-text; gloss-self-verify} at matched budget, R1+R3 minimum, full F0 metric vector V with the cost_ratio efficiency headline; later modes (L1a canonicalisation, L3b routed-hybrid, grounding-lint) join only after their own rung PASSes. Reporting: coverage-first, blended Δ_B = κ_B × Δ_covered stated as a derived identity (off-slice is byte-identical by construction), selection-effect disclosure (model-alone acc checkable vs non-checkable), NICHE-SCOPE banner below thresholds.
epistemic_refs: measured:d-ext lemma-touch 2903/5957 (~49%) vs item-checkability 0/500 (data/d-ext/manifest.json) — checkability, not vocabulary, is the binding constraint
measured:m0b coverage 0.3542 @ molecules-v0 (corpus-indexed, TinyStories-validation; extrapolates to no benchmark)
verdict:l3a (engine-oracle PASS, gold-parse only — no NL behaviour licensed)

requires: f2b-transfer PASS (for the verify-retry mode's seat; a FAIL re-gates LB-1 on the next passed mode, L3b or L1a)
compatibility.conflicts_note: Premature to RUN at current coverage — already measured, not conjectured: d-ext shows the best supported config is byte-identical to model-alone on a real public benchmark (0/500 checkable), so a naive run measures coverage, not kernel quality, and reads as misleadingly 'bad'. Value theses stay CORRECTNESS + EFFICIENCY on the checkable slice. The b-cov census part is NOT premature: Tier 0, ~$0, now-eligible, and is itself the coverage-growth instrument.

open_questions: WiC sourcing: pre-declared rank-1 lexical benchmark is unavailable unauthenticated (pilehvar/wic HTTP 401, d-ext preference walk) — authenticated HF sourcing or original distribution, fail-closed on provenance.

LB-GATE thresholds (κ_B >= 0.10 headline / 0.20 banner-free; >=500 checkable items on >=2 benchmarks) are STIPULATED design constants mirroring m0b X=20% and f2b powering — maintainer ratifies or resets at freeze.
Benchmark contamination in pretraining mixtures: carried as the accepted RT-7a limitation (eval splits first) — is a stronger decontamination step worth its cost at LB-1 freeze?
When do L3b (engine answers checkable items directly — likely efficiency winner on simple-factual slices) and L1a modes join? Own rung gates, never discovered at LB-1.
Census-vs-runtime predicate drift: LB-1 pre-declares an engagement floor on the census-checkable slice; disagreement is INSTRUMENT-INVALID (fix the census, never reinterpret).

blocked_on: LB-GATE census yield (>=500 checkable items on each of >=2 external benchmarks)
feasibility.now_eligible_part: b-cov census: Tier 0, ~$0, CPU, this box — no gate

notes: SCOPED by Fable 2026-07-09 (bead kernel-of-truth-ou3): full design + placement in docs/next/architecture-ladder.md §10 (N1-LB). Answers to the stub's open questions: (1) coverage milestone = LB-GATE (§10.4): f2b-TRANSFER PASS + census-measured >=500 checkable items on >=2 benchmarks + κ thresholds 0.10/0.20 + maintainer sign-off; evaluated on b-cov output, never estimated. (2) triage (§10.2, STIPULATED prior, census decides): Tier A = WiC / OpenBookQA / ARC-Easy / CommonsenseQA / TruthfulQA-mc / BoolQ / MMLU definitional-heavy subjects per-subject; Tier C essentially-none = GSM8K/MATH, HellaSwag, WinoGrande, code, broad trivia, multi-hop. (3) modes (§10.3): verify-retry first (only MEASURED mechanism, contingent on f2b-TRANSFER), L3b/L1a/grounding-lint join only after their own rung PASSes; mandatory nulls inherited from f2b (kernel-as-text, gloss-self-verify, shuffled-kernel). (4) reporting (§10.5): coverage-first, blended-score identity Δ_B = κ_B × Δ_covered descriptive-only, forbidden 'improves <benchmark>' phrasing with a licensed template, selection-effect disclosure, trajectory-is-the-deliverable, NICHE-SCOPE banner. Placement: NOT a ladder rung — a recurring coverage-gated measurement surface tied to the efficiency track (F0 accounting, cost_ratio headline); b-cov now, LB-1 post-gate, recurs per kernel version. Do NOT run LB-1 at current coverage.
